serStmt.serInfo: Statistics South Africa. General Household Survey 2013 [dataset]. Version 1.2. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2017. Cape Town. DataFirst [distributor], 2020. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/a704-wm31

verStmt.version: v1.2: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes:
Version 1:
Weights in General Household Survey 2013 v1 are based on figures provided by the 2013 mid-year population estimation model that incorporates the demographic findings of Census 2012. Household files were weighted independently of person files.
Version 1.1:
GHS 2013 version 1.1 includes revised weights. This version was released at the same time as GHS 2017 (21 June 2018). It was decided to replace the 2013 series mid-year population estimation in the previous version with a the more recent 2017 series mid-year population estimation as benchmarks for weighting the GHS data files. Household files were weighted independently of person files.
In the version 1.1 of the GHS 2013 household file, the variable "UqNr" (unique identifier for households) does not uniquely identify households.
Version 1.2:
As noted in the version note for v1.1, the household identifier in the household file does not uniquely identify households. This was fixed by DataFirst. The new household file has therefore been versioned at v1.2. Note that DataFirst version at file level.
In the household file v1.2, you can find two identifiers for the households. The first one is called “UqNr” - this is the revised and unique household identifier. The second one is called “UqNr_error”, this is the erroneous uqnr that does not uniquely identify the GHS households. Both variables are included in the datafile for users. For more information, please contact DataFirst.

stdyInfo.abstract: The GHS is an annual household survey specifically designed to measure the living circumstances of South African households. The GHS collects data on education, health and social development, housing, household access to services and facilities, food security, and agriculture.

sumDscr.geogCover: The survey is representative at national level and at provincial level.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregations is province. The information of geo-type and metro is also present in the data.
sumDscr.universe: The survey covers all de jure household members (usual residents) of households in the nine provinces of South Africa and residents in workers' hostels. The survey does not cover collective living quarters such as student hostels, old age homes, hospitals, prisons and military barracks.

stdyInfo.notes:
The scope of GHS includes:
Demographic information: name, sex, age, population group, etc.
Biographic information: education, health, disability, welfare
Tourism information: non-remunerated trips undertaken in the 12 months prior to the survey.
Household information: type of dwelling, ownership of dwelling and other assets, electricity, water and sanitation, environmental issues, services, transport, expenditure etc.

dataColl.sampProc:
The sample design for the GHS 2013 was based on a master sample (MS) that was originally designed for the Quarterly Labour Force Survey (QLFS) and was used for the first time for the GHS in 2008. This master sample is shared by the QLFS, GHS, Living Conditions Survey (LCS), Domestic Tourism Survey (DTS) and the Income and Expenditure Survey (IES).
The master sample used a two-stage, stratified design with probability-proportional-to-size (PPS) sampling of primary sampling units (PSUs) from within strata, and systematic sampling of dwelling units (DUs) from the sampled PSUs. A self-weighting design at provincial level was used and MS stratification was divided into two levels. Primary stratification was defined by metropolitan and non-metropolitan geographic area type. During secondary stratification, the Census 2001 data were summarised at PSU level. The following variables were used for secondary stratification: household size, education, occupancy status, gender, industry and income.
dataColl.resInstru: GHS uses questionnaires as data collection instruments
dataColl.collSitu: The General Household Survey was conducted in July of each year from 2002 to 2008. From 2009-2012 the data was collected between July-September. From 2013 onwards Statistics South Africa changed their Social Statistics surveys to continuous collection (January-December) to better account for seasonality in surveys such as the Domestic Tourism Survey, use resources more efficiently, and work towards greater integration of the GHS with, for example the Living Conditions Survey and the Income and Expenditure Survey. In the case of collection taking place over more than one month, bench-marking would take place to the middle of that period, e.g. July - September collection was benchmarked to mid-August. January - December collection would be benchmarked to the mid-year estimates.
dataColl.weight: The sampling weights for the data collected from the sampled households were constructed so that the responses could be properly expanded to represent the entire civilian population of South Africa. The design weights, which are the inverse sampling rate (ISR) for the province, are assigned to each of the households in a province. These were adjusted for four factors: Informal PSUs, Growth PSUs, Sample Stabilisation, and Non-responding Units.records.

anlyInfo.dataAppr:
In GHS 2009-2015:
The variable on land size in the General Household Survey questionnaire for 2009-2015 should be used with caution. The data comes from questions on the households' agricultural activities in Section 8 of the GHS questionnaire: Household Livelihoods: Agricultural Activities. Question 8.8b asks:
“Approximately how big is the land that the household use for production? Estimate total area if more than one piece.” One of the response category is worded as:
1 = Less than 500m2 (approximately one soccer field)
However, a soccer field is 5000 m2, not 500, therefore response category 1 is incorrect. The correct category option should be 5000 sqm. This response option is correct for GHS 2002-2008 and was flagged and corrected by Statistics SA in the GHS 2016.

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. General Household Survey 2013 [dataset]. Version 1.2. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2017. Cape Town. DataFirst [distributor], 2020. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/a704-wm31
useStmt.conditions: Public use files, available to all

var.name: UqNr
var.labl: Unique identifier for the household, fixed by DataFirst (15 Nov 2020)
var.imputation: Unique identifier for the household, fixed by DataFirst (15 Nov 2020)
var.security: Unique identifier for the household, fixed by DataFirst (15 Nov 2020)
var.embargo: Unique identifier for the household, fixed by DataFirst (15 Nov 2020)
var.respUnit: Unique identifier for the household, fixed by DataFirst (15 Nov 2020)

Traditional dwelling/hut/structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Room/flatlet on a property or a larger dwelling servants' quarters/granny flat

var.imputation: Place where children spent most of their time during the day
var.security: Place where children spent most of their time during the day
var.embargo: Place where children spent most of their time during the day
var.respUnit: Place where children spent most of their time during the day

catgry.labl: At home with parent, foster parent or guardian
At home with someone younger than 18 years
Has completed education/satisfied with my level of education/do not want to study
He or she is working at home or business/job
Education is useless or not interesting
Pre-school (including day care, crèche, pre-primary, ECD centre,
School (including Grade R/Grade 0 learners who attended a formal
Adult Education and Training Learning Centre (AET Centre)
Other than any of the above
Minibus/bus provided by institution/government and not paid for
Vehicle hired by a group of parents
Own car or other private vehicle

var.name: Q115dNNI
var.labl: Reason not attending the nearest institution
var.imputation: Reason not attending the nearest institution
var.security: Reason not attending the nearest institution
var.embargo: Reason not attending the nearest institution
var.respUnit: Reason not attending the nearest institution

var.name: Q116FEET
var.labl: Total amount of tuition fees paid
var.imputation: Total amount of tuition fees paid
var.security: Total amount of tuition fees paid
var.embargo: Total amount of tuition fees paid
var.respUnit: Total amount of tuition fees paid

var.name: Q119cTABS
var.labl: Teachers are often absent from school
var.imputation: Teachers are often absent from school
var.security: Teachers are often absent from school
var.embargo: Teachers are often absent from school
var.respUnit: Teachers are often absent from school

var.name: Q119cPMAN
var.labl: Teachers were involved in a strike
var.imputation: Teachers were involved in a strike
var.security: Teachers were involved in a strike
var.embargo: Teachers were involved in a strike
var.respUnit: Teachers were involved in a strike

catgry.labl: Yes, a few times a week

var.name: Q123aVIOL
var.labl: Violence, corporal punishment or verbal abuse
var.imputation: Violence, corporal punishment or verbal abuse
var.security: Violence, corporal punishment or verbal abuse
var.embargo: Violence, corporal punishment or verbal abuse
var.respUnit: Violence, corporal punishment or verbal abuse

catgry.labl: Not applicable - school closed e.g. school holiday
Did not want to go to school
Need to take care of someone else at home
Does not feel safe at school

var.name: Q23bFLU
var.labl: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection
var.imputation: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection
var.security: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection
var.embargo: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection
var.respUnit: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection

var.name: Q23bTBC
var.labl: TB or severe cough with blood
var.imputation: TB or severe cough with blood
var.security: TB or severe cough with blood
var.embargo: TB or severe cough with blood
var.respUnit: TB or severe cough with blood

var.name: Q24aCON
var.labl: Consult a health worker as a result of illness
var.imputation: Consult a health worker as a result of illness
var.security: Consult a health worker as a result of illness
var.embargo: Consult a health worker as a result of illness
var.respUnit: Consult a health worker as a result of illness

var.name: Q24bYNT
var.labl: Reason for not consulting a health worker
var.imputation: Reason for not consulting a health worker
var.security: Reason for not consulting a health worker
var.embargo: Reason for not consulting a health worker
var.respUnit: Reason for not consulting a health worker

catgry.labl: Not necessary/the problem was not serious enough
Experienced difficulty getting a diagnosis before

var.name: Q25aInjuries
var.labl: Consult a health worker as a result of injury
var.imputation: Consult a health worker as a result of injury
var.security: Consult a health worker as a result of injury
var.embargo: Consult a health worker as a result of injury
var.respUnit: Consult a health worker as a result of injury

catgry.labl: Severe trauma due to violence, assault, beating
The child has been born alive
The child died in the womb or during childbirth after the 7th month of pregnancy (stillbirth)
The child died in the womb or the pregnancy ended before the 7th month of pregnancy (spontaneous abortion/miscarriage)
The pregnancy was ended by choice before the child was born (termination of pregnancy/abortion by choice)
The nominated care giver (Living with child)
Someone outside the household, but most of the money is transferred to this household
Someone outside the household and most of the money is used outside this household
Someone outside the household, half of the money is used inside this household and half outside

var.name: Q34CCG
var.labl: Visits by the community care givers
var.imputation: Visits by the community care givers
var.security: Visits by the community care givers
var.embargo: Visits by the community care givers
var.respUnit: Visits by the community care givers

var.name: Q34DMVL
var.labl: Services for victims of domestic violence
var.imputation: Services for victims of domestic violence
var.security: Services for victims of domestic violence
var.embargo: Services for victims of domestic violence
var.respUnit: Services for victims of domestic violence

var.name: Q34DRUG
var.labl: Social work services for drug abuse
var.imputation: Social work services for drug abuse
var.security: Social work services for drug abuse
var.embargo: Social work services for drug abuse
var.respUnit: Social work services for drug abuse

var.name: Q41aWGE
var.labl: Working for a wage, commission or salary
var.imputation: Working for a wage, commission or salary
var.security: Working for a wage, commission or salary
var.embargo: Working for a wage, commission or salary
var.respUnit: Working for a wage, commission or salary

catgry.labl: Lift club by a group of people sharing a private vehicle

var.name: Q44bMIN
var.labl: Time taken to place of employment
var.imputation: Time taken to place of employment
var.security: Time taken to place of employment
var.embargo: Time taken to place of employment
var.respUnit: Time taken to place of employment

catgry.labl: In the formal sector (registered to perform activity)
In the informal sector (not registered to perform activity)

var.name: Q46aLOOK
var.labl: Looking for any kind of job or trying business
var.imputation: Looking for any kind of job or trying business
var.security: Looking for any kind of job or trying business
var.embargo: Looking for any kind of job or trying business
var.respUnit: Looking for any kind of job or trying business

var.imputation: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business
var.security: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business
var.embargo: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business
var.respUnit: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business

var.embargo: Participated in Government or municipal job creation programme
var.respUnit: Participated in Government or municipal job creation programme

catgry.labl: Acentral, tribal, animist, or other traditional African religions
Usually at least once a week
Usually once or twice a month
Usually a few times a year
